frameworks.name: fitness-maximizing neural encoding
frameworks.one_line: early sensory codes are tuned to maximize reward expectation rather than representational accuracy

frameworks.name: accurate sensory representation
frameworks.one_line: encoding that minimizes reconstruction error of the sensory signal

frameworks.name: unified normative framework
frameworks.one_line: combines neuroscience, computer science, and economics to predict reward-driven codes

core_question: Do early sensory neural codes maximize organism fitness rather than representational accuracy?
key_assumptions: encoding can be evaluated against an organismal fitness/reward objective
biological coding limits constrain feasible representations
reward structure imposed by environment shapes peripheral coding
manipulations: comparison of fitness-maximizing vs accuracy-maximizing predictions
reward/fitness structure across retinotopic locations in humans
key_findings: blowfly LMC retinal responses match reward-maximizing codes, not accurate representations
human early sensory areas adopt fitness-promoting codes in a retinotopically-specific way
these encoding changes impact decision behavior
interpretations: fitness-maximizing rules from the environment act at the earliest stages of sensory processing
sensory coding is best understood as reward-driven rather than fidelity-driven
